prs.title: Replace Camera Icon with Barcode Icon for QR Scanner Button
prs.body: The QR scanner button previously displayed a camera icon (mdi--camera), which was not the most intuitive choice for barcode scanning. This PR updates the button to use a barcode icon (mdi--barcode-scan) for better clarity and user experience. Changes Made: Replaced with Ensured the new icon aligns correctly with the button style Why this change? The barcode icon better represents the functionality of scanning a product barcode. Enhances usability by making the button's purpose immediately clear. Testing: Manually verified that the barcode icon displays correctly in the UI. Ensured no other UI elements were affected.

prs.title: Fixed QR Scanner Box Size Not Updating on Window Resize
prs.body:
### Issue
The QR code scanner dynamically adjusts its qrbox size based on screen width when the component mounts. However, if the window is resized after the initial load, the scanner does not update its qrbox size accordingly. This can cause suboptimal scanning areas, especially when switching between mobile and desktop views.

### Fixes bug
Fixed QR scanner responsiveness: The qrbox size now dynamically updates when the window is resized, ensuring optimal scanning areas across different screen sizes."

prs.title: 6. ZigZag Conversion
prs.body:
This pull request implements the solution for the Zigzag Conversion problem as described in LeetCode.

Problem Statement:
Given a string and a number of rows (numRows), the string is written in a zigzag pattern across multiple rows and then read row-by-row.


Example:
Input: "PAYPALISHIRING", numRows = 3
Output: "PAHNAPLSIIGYIR"


Approach:
The solution simulates the zigzag pattern by iterating through the string and placing characters into the corresponding rows in a zigzag order.
The characters are then read row-by-row to construct the final string.
Edge Cases Considered:
If numRows is 1, the original string is returned since no zigzag conversion is needed.
The algorithm handles strings shorter than or equal to numRows.

prs.title: Added a random color palette generator project
prs.body:
## What does this PR do?

This pull request introduces a Random Color Palette Generator project using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The main functionality allows users to generate a random set of color swatches and copy the color code to the clipboard by clicking on any swatch.

Key Features:

Random Color Generation: Each time the "Generate Palette" button is clicked, five new random colors are displayed.
Interactive Swatches: Hover effect for each color swatch, and the color code is shown on hover.
Copy to Clipboard: When a user clicks on any color swatch, the corresponding color code is copied to the clipboard, and a confirmation alert is shown.
Technical Details:

The getRandomColor function generates random hexadecimal color codes.
The color swatches are dynamically created and inserted into the DOM using JavaScript.
Basic responsive design with Flexbox for the layout of the color palette.
Minimalist CSS styling for a clean and modern look.

### Have you read the [Contributing Guidelines on issues](CONTRIBUTING.md)?

Yes

prs.title: Added a new project -  Age Calculator
prs.body:
## What does this PR do?

This Pull Request adds a new project called Age Calculator to the repository. The Age Calculator is a web application that allows users to input their date of birth and calculates their current age in years, months, and days.

Key features of this project include:

User-Friendly Interface: The application presents a clean and intuitive layout, making it easy for users to enter their date of birth.
Responsive Design: The design is optimized for various screen sizes, ensuring a good experience on both desktop and mobile devices.
Interactive Elements: The calculator includes interactive buttons with hover effects and input fields that highlight when focused, enhancing user engagement.
Clear Output: Once the age is calculated, the result is displayed prominently, providing users with immediate feedback.

### Have you read the [Contributing Guidelines on issues](CONTRIBUTING.md)?

Yes

prs.title: Fixed menu button functionality by updating from getElementsByClassName to querySelector
prs.body:
Issue Fixed:

Fixed a JavaScript bug that caused the menu button functionality to break.
Changes Made:

Replaced getElementsByClassName with querySelector to ensure the correct handling of single elements when adding event listeners.
Updated the script for the menu button to toggle the active class correctly.

Impact:
The menu button now works as expected, toggling the active class when clicked, fixing the broken navigation on the website.

issues.title: QR Scanner Box Size Not Updating on Window Resize
issues.body:
### What
The QR code scanner dynamically adjusts its qrbox size based on screen width when the component mounts. However, if the window is resized after the initial load, the scanner does not update its qrbox size accordingly. This can cause suboptimal scanning areas, especially when switching between mobile and desktop views.

### Steps to reproduce the behavior:
Open the QR scanner page on a desktop and observe the qrbox size.
Resize the window to a mobile view (less than 640px width).
Notice that the scanner still uses the old dimensions and does not adjust dynamically.

### Expected behavior
The qrbox size should dynamically adjust when the window is resized to match the updated screen width.

### Platform (Desktop, Mobile)
Browser: (Chrome, Safari)
OS: (macOS, Android)

Pull Request
The issue has been fixed in the following PR: [#233](https://github.com/openfoodfacts/openfoodfacts-explorer/pull/233)

issues.title: No Indication When Camera is Disabled for QR Code Scanner
issues.body:
**### What**
When the camera is disabled and the user clicks "Scan QR Code", there is no feedback or message indicating that camera access is required. This can cause confusion, as users may not realize they need to enable the camera manually.

**### Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Disable the camera in browser settings.
2. Open the app and click "Scan QR Code".
3. Nothing happens—no error message, no prompt to enable the camera.

**### Expected behavior**
A message should inform users that camera access is required.
Optionally, add a "Go to Settings" button to guide users.

**Show an alert or pop-up like:**
"Camera access is disabled. Please enable it in browser settings to use the QR scanner."
Optionally, add a button:
"Go to Settings" → On mobile, open app permissions.
(In Svelte, direct redirection isn't possible, but we can guide users manually with instructions.)

**### Browser & OS Tested On**
 Chrome / Safari
 macOS / Android

**Screenshots/Video:**
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c6b28080-5404-4f40-a4fb-6804daad2379
